title:
Guidance
	 - data.govt.nz
content:
It can be difficult to navigate the numerous guidance tools, frameworks, and principles out there. Here, we have outlined the key guidance in Aotearoa, how they may best apply to your project and direct you to the practical tools for each guidance.
What you’ll find here is:
- Algorithm Charter and the Algorithm Impact Assessment (AIA) The Charter provides a framework to understand high risk algorithms, while the AIA toolkit provides practical tools.
- Data Protection and Use Policy This guidance is a good option for anyone who works directly with service users or communities.
- The Privacy, Human Rights and Ethics (PHRaE) Framework This guidance is a good option if privacy is the number one priority for your product, service, system or process.
- Ngā Tikanga Paihere This guidance is a good option if you want Te Ao Māori principles to inform your data practice. It is also a good framework for thinking about working with communities, and ensuring your data practices occur in good faith.
- Artificial Intelligence Guidance We’ve collated a selection of good pieces of AI guidance you may like to consider for your project.
- Information Sharing Resource Hub This guidance is a good option for anyone who needs to share information regularly, especially personal information.
The Centre is currently developing a guidance library, which will include case studies, guides, and tools. Watch this space.
If you can’t find the guidance you need for your project, please feel free to email us as we may be able to provide further support.
